Title: Phytochrome B stabilizes the KNOX transcription factor BP/KNAT1 to promote light-initiated seed germination in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant (phyB-9); overexpression (35S:phyB-Flag); genetic interaction with 35S:BP-GUS; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; immunoblot; RNA-seq comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increased in imbibed seeds under phyB-on conditions relative to phyB-off as discussed; phyB active in imbibed seeds after FR/R treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in imbibed seeds during red light-triggered germination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phyB is required for efficient light-initiated seed germination under FR/R conditions. 2. phyB acts upstream of BP and directly interacts with BP, with stronger interaction under phyB-on conditions. 3. phyB promotes BP accumulation by reducing BP ubiquitination and proteasome-mediated degradation. 4. Through the phyB-BP-NCED6/9 module, phyB represses ABA biosynthesis and promotes germination.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-initiated seed germination by stabilizing BP and lowering ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and promotes seed dormancy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Far-red/red light regimes; phyB-on (FR/R) versus phyB-off (FR); dark incubation after light pulse
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: phyB-9 shows poor germination; no broader growth penalty of phyB manipulation evaluated in this paper
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve light-responsive germination by modulating the phyB-BP-ABA module, though direct crop application not tested
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although phyB influences hormone metabolism, it is a photoreceptor/signaling protein rather than a metabolic enzyme or TF; classified here as Common Gene. phyB partially controls BP both post-translationally and transcriptionally.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family; NCED enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step of carotenoid-derived ABA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a direct ABA precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/rate-limiting ABA biosynthetic enzyme in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression (35S:NCED6-GFP); loss-of-function mutant; double mutant with nced9; genetic crosses with BP overexpression and bp mutant; RT-qPCR; germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in imbibed seeds; transcript strongly repressed by BP under phyB-on conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Key ABA biosynthetic gene in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED6 is a negative regulator of phyB-dependent seed germination. 2. NCED6 transcript is elevated in bp mutants under phyB-on conditions. 3. BP directly binds NCED6 regulatory sequence and represses its transcription. 4. NCED6 overexpression reduces germination and suppresses the promotive effect of BP overexpression. 5. nced6 nced9 is epistatic to bp for germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA accumulation and suppresses light-initiated seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and inhibits seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: FR/R light treatment activating phyB; dark incubation after light pulse
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces germination under light-inductive conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulation/editing of NCED6 may enhance germination by reducing ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with NCED9, NCED6 functions downstream of phyB-BP signaling to control seed ABA levels.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family; NCED enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step of carotenoid-derived ABA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a direct ABA precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key/rate-limiting ABA biosynthetic enzyme in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; double mutant with nced6; genetic analysis with bp; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase repression assay; ABA measurement and germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in imbibed seeds; transcript repressed by BP under phyB-on conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Key ABA biosynthetic gene in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED9 is directly repressed by BP in imbibed seeds. 2. BP binds TGAC motif-containing region of NCED9 in vitro and in vivo. 3. H3K27me3 levels at NCED9 are promoted by BP and reduced in bp mutant. 4. Along with NCED6, NCED9 acts downstream of BP to promote ABA accumulation and suppress germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA accumulation and suppresses light-initiated seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains dormancy and inhibits seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: FR/R light treatment activating phyB; dark incubation after light pulse
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely reduced dormancy when downregulated, though not explicitly quantified independently here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression/editing of NCED9, ideally with NCED6, could reduce ABA and enhance germination
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: BP-dependent repression is associated with increased H3K27me3 at NCED9 chromatin; H3K27me3 decreases in bp-9
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed primarily together with NCED6 as a redundant module downstream of BP.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (inferred for transcription factor; direct localization not explicitly tested in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutants (bp-9, bp-1); complementation with native promoter BP-GFP; overexpression (35S:BP-GUS); genetic interaction with phyB-9 and nced6 nced9; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; ABA quantification; ubiquitination and proteasome assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms BP homodimers; may associate with chromatin modifiers to regulate H3K27me3, but PRC2 component not identified here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in roots and stems; moderate in flowers and siliques; undetectable in leaves; transcript induced in imbibed seeds under phyB-on conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in imbibed seeds during FR/R-induced germination; expression/transcript accumulation promoted by phyB-on conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis and ABA-associated germination regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses NCED6 and NCED9 transcription; also represses ABA signaling genes ABI3, ABI4, and ABI5 indirectly or as downstream consequence
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct DNA binding to TGAC motif-containing regions and transcriptional repression associated with increased H3K27me3 deposition; protein stabilization by phyB through reduced ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of light-induced germination by serving as a direct repressor of key ABA biosynthetic genes downstream of phyB
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BP is a positive regulator of phyB-dependent seed germination. 2. BP acts genetically downstream of phyB, and BP overexpression partially rescues phyB mutant germination. 3. BP directly interacts with phyB, which stabilizes BP by decreasing its ubiquitination and 26S proteasome-mediated degradation. 4. BP represses NCED6 and NCED9 by direct promoter/exon binding and increased H3K27me3, reducing ABA accumulation in imbibed seeds. 5. nced6 nced9 is epistatic to bp, placing NCED6/9 downstream of BP.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-initiated seed germination by lowering ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and promotes dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: FR/R red-light activation of phyB versus FR phyB-off; MG132 and CHX used in protein stability assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S:BP-GUS line exhibits significantly smaller plant size relative to wild type
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising regulatory target for tuning germination via ABA suppression, but constitutive overexpression may incur growth penalties; seed-specific or inducible manipulation would be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: BP represses NCED6/NCED9 expression by increasing H3K27me3 levels at these loci; H3K4me3 was not significantly affected in this context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BP does not directly interact with PIF1 in yeast; BP is proposed to function independently of the phyB-PIF1-SOM cascade in repressing NCED6/9.
